rules.reason: An unspecified or non-standard payment method may create uncertainty over payment timing, banking documents, and remedies for non-payment.
rules.followUpQuestion: What exact payment process, documents, and payment deadline will apply to this transaction?
rules.reviewPoint: Clarify the payment mechanism, required documents, payment deadline, default interest, and suspension rights.
rules.legalRationale: Trade-based money laundering indicators may include third-party payments, unusual payment routes, mismatched parties, vague goods descriptions, unusual pricing, and unclear source of funds.
rules.applicabilityLimits: Risk indicators are not conclusive proof of money laundering. They require further due diligence and manual verification.
evidenceNeeded: Buyer, payer, and beneficiary relationship explanation
rules.recommendedAction: Verify payer-buyer relationship, source of funds, beneficial ownership, payment route, and document consistency before accepting payment.
rules.escalation: Escalate to compliance/finance/legal if payment parties, routes, documents, or source of funds remain unclear.

en.title: Payment Risk
en.explanation: An unspecified or non-standard payment method may create uncertainty over payment timing, banking documents, and remedies for non-payment.

zh.title: Payment Risk
zh.explanation: An unspecified or non-standard payment method may create uncertainty over payment timing, banking documents, and remedies for non-payment.

rules.reason: Without clear Incoterms, delivery obligations, cost allocation, and risk transfer may be unclear.
rules.followUpQuestion: Which Incoterms rule applies to this transaction?
rules.reviewPoint: Clarify the delivery term, place of delivery, insurance obligation, customs responsibility, and transfer of risk.
rules.legalRationale: Delivery terms should be reviewed because they allocate delivery obligations, costs, customs responsibilities, and risk transfer.
rules.applicabilityLimits: Incoterms do not decide title transfer, payment obligations, governing law, dispute resolution, sanctions, or export control compliance.
rules.recommendedAction: Clarify the Incoterms rule, named place/port, insurance responsibility, customs clearance responsibility, and logistics workflow.
rules.escalation: Escalate to legal/logistics review if delivery responsibility, risk transfer, insurance, or customs clearance is unclear.

en.title: Incoterms / Delivery Risk
en.explanation: Without clear Incoterms, delivery obligations, cost allocation, and risk transfer may be unclear.

zh.title: Incoterms / Delivery Risk
zh.explanation: Without clear Incoterms, delivery obligations, cost allocation, and risk transfer may be unclear.

rules.reason: No Incoterms rule has been selected, which may leave the parties' delivery and risk allocation obligations uncertain.
rules.followUpQuestion: Should the parties use EXW, FCA, FOB, CIF, DAP, DDP, or another Incoterms rule?
rules.reviewPoint: Align the selected Incoterms rule with logistics, insurance, customs clearance capability, and tax considerations.
rules.legalRationale: Delivery terms should be reviewed because they allocate delivery obligations, costs, customs responsibilities, and risk transfer.
rules.applicabilityLimits: Incoterms do not decide title transfer, payment obligations, governing law, dispute resolution, sanctions, or export control compliance.
rules.recommendedAction: Clarify the Incoterms rule, named place/port, insurance responsibility, customs clearance responsibility, and logistics workflow.
rules.escalation: Escalate to legal/logistics review if delivery responsibility, risk transfer, insurance, or customs clearance is unclear.

en.title: Incoterms / Delivery Risk
en.explanation: No Incoterms rule has been selected, which may leave the parties' delivery and risk allocation obligations uncertain.

zh.title: Incoterms / Delivery Risk
zh.explanation: No Incoterms rule has been selected, which may leave the parties' delivery and risk allocation obligations uncertain.

rules.reason: Sanctions screening has not been conducted, creating potential restricted-party, destination, vessel, bank, or payment blocking risk.
rules.followUpQuestion: Have the buyer, seller, beneficial owners, banks, vessels, and destination been screened against relevant sanctions lists?
rules.reviewPoint: Conduct and document sanctions and restricted-party screening before contract execution, shipment, and payment.
rules.legalRationale: Sanctions review may be required for counterparties, beneficial owners, banks, vessels, destinations, and payment channels, especially where a UN, EU, UK, U.S., USD, or restricted-region nexus may exist.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not query live sanctions lists. Restricted-region examples are illustrative and non-exhaustive. Manual verification against current official lists is required.
evidenceNeeded: Vessel, port, destination, and end-user information
rules.recommendedAction: Conduct and document manual sanctions screening against current official databases before signing, shipment, payment, or performance.
rules.escalation: Escalate to sanctions/compliance counsel if any name, ownership, bank, destination, vessel, or payment route raises a possible match or nexus concern.

en.title: Sanctions Risk
en.explanation: Sanctions screening has not been conducted, creating potential restricted-party, destination, vessel, bank, or payment blocking risk.

zh.title: Sanctions Risk
zh.explanation: Sanctions screening has not been conducted, creating potential restricted-party, destination, vessel, bank, or payment blocking risk.

rules.reason: It is unclear whether sanctions screening has been conducted, which may expose the transaction to undiscovered sanctions or restricted-party risk.
rules.followUpQuestion: Who is responsible for sanctions screening and what records can confirm that screening was completed?
rules.reviewPoint: Confirm screening responsibility, scope, timing, list coverage, and record retention before proceeding.
rules.legalRationale: Sanctions review may be required for counterparties, beneficial owners, banks, vessels, destinations, and payment channels, especially where a UN, EU, UK, U.S., USD, or restricted-region nexus may exist.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not query live sanctions lists. Restricted-region examples are illustrative and non-exhaustive. Manual verification against current official lists is required.
evidenceNeeded: Vessel, port, destination, and end-user information
rules.recommendedAction: Conduct and document manual sanctions screening against current official databases before signing, shipment, payment, or performance.
rules.escalation: Escalate to sanctions/compliance counsel if any name, ownership, bank, destination, vessel, or payment route raises a possible match or nexus concern.

en.title: Sanctions Risk
en.explanation: It is unclear whether sanctions screening has been conducted, which may expose the transaction to undiscovered sanctions or restricted-party risk.

zh.title: Sanctions Risk
zh.explanation: It is unclear whether sanctions screening has been conducted, which may expose the transaction to undiscovered sanctions or restricted-party risk.

rules.reason: The product may be subject to export control, which may require classification, end-use review, licensing, or contractual compliance commitments.
rules.followUpQuestion: Has the product been classified and have the end user, destination, and end use been verified?
rules.reviewPoint: Assess export control classification, destination restrictions, end-use statements, license requirements, and compliance clauses.
rules.legalRationale: Dual-use goods, controlled technology, technical data, sensitive end users, and sensitive end uses may require classification and licensing review.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not classify products, determine ECCNs, check control lists, or determine license requirements. Manual export control review is required.
rules.recommendedAction: Collect product and end-use materials, classify the item, verify end user/end use, and assess license requirements before shipment or technical transfer.
rules.escalation: Escalate to export control counsel if the item, technology, destination, end user, or end use is unknown or sensitive.

en.title: Export Control Risk
en.explanation: The product may be subject to export control, which may require classification, end-use review, licensing, or contractual compliance commitments.

zh.title: Export Control Risk
zh.explanation: The product may be subject to export control, which may require classification, end-use review, licensing, or contractual compliance commitments.

rules.reason: The export control status of the product is unknown, which may delay shipment or create compliance exposure.
rules.followUpQuestion: What product specifications, HS code, technical parameters, and end-use information are available for classification?
rules.reviewPoint: Collect product information and perform export control classification before shipment or technology transfer.
rules.legalRationale: Dual-use goods, controlled technology, technical data, sensitive end users, and sensitive end uses may require classification and licensing review.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not classify products, determine ECCNs, check control lists, or determine license requirements. Manual export control review is required.
rules.recommendedAction: Collect product and end-use materials, classify the item, verify end user/end use, and assess license requirements before shipment or technical transfer.
rules.escalation: Escalate to export control counsel if the item, technology, destination, end user, or end use is unknown or sensitive.

en.title: Export Control Risk
en.explanation: The export control status of the product is unknown, which may delay shipment or create compliance exposure.

zh.title: Export Control Risk
zh.explanation: The export control status of the product is unknown, which may delay shipment or create compliance exposure.

rules.reason: The contract appears to lack an anti-bribery clause, which may weaken controls over improper payments, gifts, agents, and public-sector touchpoints.
rules.followUpQuestion: Does the transaction involve agents, distributors, customs brokers, or government-facing interactions?
rules.reviewPoint: Add compliance representations, anti-bribery undertakings, audit rights, termination rights, and reporting obligations.
rules.legalRationale: Intermediaries, unusual commissions, government customers, state-owned enterprises, gifts, hospitality, and facilitation payments may require anti-bribery due diligence.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not determine whether bribery occurred or whether FCPA/UK Bribery Act jurisdiction applies. Manual legal review is required.
evidenceNeeded: Gifts, hospitality, or facilitation payment records
rules.recommendedAction: Add anti-bribery undertakings, conduct third-party due diligence, review commission terms, and document approvals.
rules.escalation: Escalate to compliance/legal if an intermediary, public-sector touchpoint, unusual commission, gift, or hospitality issue appears.

en.title: Contract Clause Risk
en.explanation: The contract appears to lack an anti-bribery clause, which may weaken controls over improper payments, gifts, agents, and public-sector touchpoints.

zh.title: Contract Clause Risk
zh.explanation: The contract appears to lack an anti-bribery clause, which may weaken controls over improper payments, gifts, agents, and public-sector touchpoints.

rules.reason: Without a dispute resolution clause, forum, procedure, language, interim relief, and enforceability may be uncertain.
rules.followUpQuestion: Should disputes be resolved by court litigation or arbitration, and in which forum or seat?
rules.reviewPoint: Draft a dispute resolution clause covering forum, seat or jurisdiction, language, institution if any, interim measures, and enforcement strategy.
rules.legalRationale: Cross-border disputes require planning for forum, procedure, language, interim relief, and enforcement.
rules.applicabilityLimits: This demo does not assess enforceability in a specific jurisdiction. Manual legal review is required.
rules.recommendedAction: Specify forum or arbitration, seat, institution, language, interim measures, and enforcement strategy.
rules.escalation: Escalate to legal review before signing if forum, seat, institution, or enforcement path is unclear.

en.title: Dispute Resolution Risk
en.explanation: Without a dispute resolution clause, forum, procedure, language, interim relief, and enforceability may be uncertain.

zh.title: Dispute Resolution Risk
zh.explanation: Without a dispute resolution clause, forum, procedure, language, interim relief, and enforceability may be uncertain.

rules.reason: Without a governing law clause, contract interpretation, remedies, limitation periods, and mandatory rules may be uncertain.
rules.followUpQuestion: Which governing law is commercially acceptable and enforceable for both parties?
rules.reviewPoint: Select governing law consistently with the dispute forum, performance location, mandatory rules, and enforcement considerations.
rules.riskArea: Governing law / international sale of goods
rules.legalRationale: Cross-border goods contracts may require governing law and possible CISG applicability review.
rules.applicabilityLimits: This demo does not determine CISG applicability automatically. It only flags when CISG analysis may be required.
evidenceNeeded: Buyer and seller places of business
CISG opt-in or opt-out wording if any
rules.recommendedAction: Select and document governing law, check whether CISG may apply, and align the clause with dispute resolution and enforcement planning.
rules.escalation: Escalate to legal review if the clause is missing, inconsistent, or commercially sensitive.

en.title: Governing Law Risk
en.explanation: Without a governing law clause, contract interpretation, remedies, limitation periods, and mandatory rules may be uncertain.

zh.title: Governing Law Risk
zh.explanation: Without a governing law clause, contract interpretation, remedies, limitation periods, and mandatory rules may be uncertain.

rules.reason: It is unclear whether the contract contains anti-bribery protections, which may be problematic in higher-risk sales channels or intermediary arrangements.
rules.followUpQuestion: Can the contract draft be checked for anti-bribery, sanctions, and third-party compliance wording?
rules.reviewPoint: Review compliance clauses, third-party due diligence obligations, audit rights, and termination triggers.
rules.legalRationale: Intermediaries, unusual commissions, government customers, state-owned enterprises, gifts, hospitality, and facilitation payments may require anti-bribery due diligence.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not determine whether bribery occurred or whether FCPA/UK Bribery Act jurisdiction applies. Manual legal review is required.
evidenceNeeded: Gifts, hospitality, or facilitation payment records
rules.recommendedAction: Add anti-bribery undertakings, conduct third-party due diligence, review commission terms, and document approvals.
rules.escalation: Escalate to compliance/legal if an intermediary, public-sector touchpoint, unusual commission, gift, or hospitality issue appears.

en.title: Anti-Bribery Risk
en.explanation: It is unclear whether the contract contains anti-bribery protections, which may be problematic in higher-risk sales channels or intermediary arrangements.

zh.title: Anti-Bribery Risk
zh.explanation: It is unclear whether the contract contains anti-bribery protections, which may be problematic in higher-risk sales channels or intermediary arrangements.

rules.reason: Cross-border transfer of personal data may trigger privacy and data transfer compliance requirements.
rules.followUpQuestion: What types of personal data will be transferred, and to which country or region?
rules.reviewPoint: Assess GDPR, PIPL, data processing agreements, privacy notices, security measures, and cross-border transfer mechanisms.
rules.legalRationale: EU/EEA personal data transfers outside the EU/EEA and customer data sharing with overseas agents, freight forwarders, or platforms may require transfer mechanism review.
rules.applicabilityLimits: The demo does not determine whether SCCs or any transfer mechanism are sufficient in a specific scenario. Data protection review is required.
rules.recommendedAction: Map the data flow, confirm whether personal data is involved, and review DPA/SCC, transfer mechanism, notice, and security controls.
rules.escalation: Escalate to privacy/legal review if personal data, ID/passport/payment data, or overseas processing is involved.

en.title: Cross-Border Data Risk
en.explanation: Cross-border transfer of personal data may trigger privacy and data transfer compliance requirements.

zh.title: Cross-Border Data Risk
zh.explanation: Cross-border transfer of personal data may trigger privacy and data transfer compliance requirements.

rules.reason: High-value transactions usually require stronger contract review, credit checks, insurance, approvals, and audit trail.
rules.followUpQuestion: Has this transaction been escalated for commercial, finance, compliance, and legal approval?
rules.reviewPoint: Review approval authority, credit support, liability caps, insurance coverage, tax implications, and record retention.
rules.legalRationale: High-value transactions usually require stronger contract review, credit checks, insurance, approvals, and audit trail.
rules.applicabilityLimits: This demo flags a potential review point only. It does not determine whether the transaction is lawful or unlawful.
rules.recommendedAction: Review approval authority, credit support, liability caps, insurance coverage, tax implications, and record retention.
rules.escalation: Escalate to qualified legal or compliance professionals where facts are incomplete or risk remains unclear.

en.title: High-Value Transaction Risk
en.explanation: High-value transactions usually require stronger contract review, credit checks, insurance, approvals, and audit trail.

zh.title: High-Value Transaction Risk
zh.explanation: High-value transactions usually require stronger contract review, credit checks, insurance, approvals, and audit trail.

rules.reason: Payment by a third party may indicate fraud, money laundering, or mismatch between the contracting party and the paying entity.
rules.followUpQuestion: Why is the payment made by a third party instead of the contracting buyer?
rules.reviewPoint: Verify the relationship between the buyer, payer, and beneficiary, and consider enhanced due diligence.
rules.legalRationale: Trade-based money laundering indicators may include third-party payments, unusual payment routes, mismatched parties, vague goods descriptions, unusual pricing, and unclear source of funds.
rules.applicabilityLimits: Risk indicators are not conclusive proof of money laundering. They require further due diligence and manual verification.
evidenceNeeded: Buyer, payer, and beneficiary relationship explanation
rules.recommendedAction: Verify payer-buyer relationship, source of funds, beneficial ownership, payment route, and document consistency before accepting payment.
rules.escalation: Escalate to compliance/finance/legal if payment parties, routes, documents, or source of funds remain unclear.

rules.reason: Open account payment exposes the seller to non-payment, delayed payment, and cross-border recovery risk after delivery.
rules.followUpQuestion: Has the buyer's creditworthiness been checked and are payment security measures available?
rules.reviewPoint: Consider advance payment, letter of credit, credit insurance, parent guarantee, retention of title, and suspension rights.
rules.legalRationale: Delivery terms should be reviewed because they allocate delivery obligations, costs, customs responsibilities, and risk transfer.
rules.applicabilityLimits: Incoterms do not decide title transfer, payment obligations, governing law, dispute resolution, sanctions, or export control compliance.
rules.recommendedAction: Clarify the Incoterms rule, named place/port, insurance responsibility, customs clearance responsibility, and logistics workflow.
rules.escalation: Escalate to legal/logistics review if delivery responsibility, risk transfer, insurance, or customs clearance is unclear.

zh.explanation: Open account payment exposes the seller to non-payment, delayed payment, and cross-border recovery risk after delivery.

rules.reason: Documents against acceptance may create credit exposure because payment occurs after acceptance rather than before release of documents.
rules.followUpQuestion: What is the buyer's credit profile and what happens if payment is not made at maturity?
rules.reviewPoint: Review payment maturity, bank involvement, collection procedure, default remedies, and credit support.
rules.legalRationale: Trade-based money laundering indicators may include third-party payments, unusual payment routes, mismatched parties, vague goods descriptions, unusual pricing, and unclear source of funds.
rules.applicabilityLimits: Risk indicators are not conclusive proof of money laundering. They require further due diligence and manual verification.
evidenceNeeded: Buyer, payer, and beneficiary relationship explanation
rules.recommendedAction: Verify payer-buyer relationship, source of funds, beneficial ownership, payment route, and document consistency before accepting payment.
rules.escalation: Escalate to compliance/finance/legal if payment parties, routes, documents, or source of funds remain unclear.

en.title: Open Account / Credit Risk
en.explanation: Documents against acceptance may create credit exposure because payment occurs after acceptance rather than before release of documents.

zh.title: Open Account / Credit Risk
zh.explanation: Documents against acceptance may create credit exposure because payment occurs after acceptance rather than before release of documents.
